Name: adaptive_mixed_precision_quantization
Title: Adaptive Mixed-Precision Quantization for Hardware-Focused Inference Optimization
Short Hypothesis: Can an adaptive mixed-precision quantization scheme, tailored to specific layers' performance metrics, significantly enhance throughput and reduce latency in large language models without sacrificing accuracy?
Related Work: Existing works like KVTuner and FGMP focus on layer-wise quantization but often overlook the real-time adaptability of quantization strategies based on runtime profiling. This proposal uniquely emphasizes a profiling-driven approach that continuously adapts precision based on measured performance metrics, diverging from static or pre-defined quantization schemes.
Abstract: As machine learning models, particularly large language models (LLMs), evolve, the need for efficient inference on hardware accelerators has never been more pressing. This proposal introduces an innovative adaptive mixed-precision quantization framework that leverages real-time profiling data to dynamically adjust precision levels of model layers during inference. By utilizing kernel-level throughput metrics—such as occupancy, bandwidth utilization, and arithmetic intensity—we aim to develop a system that not only optimizes per-layer precision but also adapts to the varying computational demands of different inputs. Our approach will be rigorously tested on commodity GPUs, ensuring that every claim of speedup is grounded in empirically measured performance. Preliminary experiments will involve profiling established LLMs under varying workloads and utilizing this data to inform adaptive quantization strategies. The expected outcome is a robust framework that achieves significant throughput improvements while maintaining accuracy, offering practitioners a practical tool for deploying efficient models in real-world applications.
Experiments: 1. Baseline Evaluation: Measure the performance (latency, throughput, accuracy) of standard LLMs with fixed-precision quantization (e.g., 8-bit).
2. Real-time Profiling: Implement a profiling module that collects hardware metrics during inference on various LLMs to assess layer-wise performance.

3. Adaptive Quantization: Develop an adaptive quantization algorithm that adjusts the precision of each layer based on real-time profiling data, implementing heuristics for layer sensitivity and performance.

4. Performance Comparison: Conduct A/B tests comparing the adaptive quantization model against the baseline. Evaluate metrics including p50/p99 latency, tokens-per-second, and energy consumption per token.
5. Scalability Testing: Assess the framework's scalability on different LLM sizes and architectures, ensuring that the adaptive scheme maintains efficacy across the board.
Risk Factors and Limitations: 1. Complexity in Implementation: Developing a real-time profiling and adaptive adjustment system may introduce overhead.
2. Hardware Limitations: The effectiveness of the adaptive quantization strategy may vary significantly across different hardware setups.
3. Accuracy Trade-offs: There may be unforeseen impacts on model accuracy in scenarios where precision adjustments are too aggressive.
